mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate has been falling for at least one month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive more often than not after monthly declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the return response is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%, tighter policy raises the market discount rate and compresses equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next week turn negative. The effect should appear immediately after the crossing and fade once the rate stabilizes.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered 5-day forward returns are not lower than unconditional returns, or if more than 60% of crossings are followed by nonnegative SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income growth and future profit pressure, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is directional only when unemployment is rising rather than stable.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in a majority of unemployment-rise triggers, or if the triggered average return exceeds the unconditional mean by a meaningful margin over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS indicates expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which supports equity earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect should strengthen when payroll gains are broad and persistent across consecutive months.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX forward returns are not higher after positive payroll trends than after flat or negative payroll trends, or if the sign relationship reverses for any sustained 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST remains firm, consumer spending and housing demand jointly confirm a strong domestic demand backdrop, supporting S&P 500 gains over the next month. The interaction matters because either series alone is weaker than the combined signal.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST signal does not outperform each series alone in predicting 20-day returns, or if joint-positive periods fail to deliver above-baseline SPX performance.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO indicates weakening industrial output and earnings momentum, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is most informative when production is falling for multiple consecutive months.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not worse after consecutive INDPRO declines, or if the hit rate is below 40% in triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized downturn in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals a broad demand contraction that reduces expected corporate revenue and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism requires both real output and retail activity to soften together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDP and retail sales do not predict weaker 60-day SPX performance than either proxy alone, or if joint-negative periods still generate positive forward returns most of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, rising CPIAUCSL makes a fixed FEDFUNDS rate more restrictive in real terms, so S&P 500 returns become negative over the next month when inflation is accelerating. The signal fails when inflation is stable or falling, because nominal tightening no longer sharpens real rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates for 3 consecutive months
regime_hint: inflation flare-up with static policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration episodes are not followed by below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the negative effect disappears once FEDFUNDS is held constant.
